commands.description: Traverses the element and its parents (upwards) until it finds a match for the specified selector. Essential for event delegation.
Parses specified text as HTML and inserts the resulting nodes into the DOM at a specific position ('beforebegin', 'afterbegin', 'beforeend', or 'afterend'). Faster than innerHTML for appending.
Returns a boolean indicating whether the element would be selected by the given selector string. Useful for conditional logic in event listeners.
A built-in object that provides the ability to watch for changes being made to the DOM tree (attribute changes, child additions/removals, etc.).
Returns a DOMRect object providing information about the size of an element and its position relative to the viewport. Crucial for animations and scroll logic.
